verdict_reason: Both disagreements are doctrinally substantive. (1) niśā / jāgarti / paśyataḥ — the explanation correctly identifies the perceptual-inversion-not-literal-day-night structure across all four traditions and surfaces the doctrinal axis (Shankara: paramārtha-sat vs vyāvahārika; Ramanuja: Self-as-field-of-devotion vs sense-objects; Prabhupada/Mukundananda: less specific). The note that Mukundananda glosses the first 'night' as 'night of ignorance' (adding interpretive content not present in the Sanskrit) is the kind of careful textual observation a competent reviewer would surface. (2) saṁyamī / muniḥ — the explanation correctly notes that English translators flatten both to 'sage' or 'wise', losing the chapter's careful technical vocabulary about disciplined-aspirant-vs-realised. Both explanations would survive Sanskrit-grammarian + comparative-philosophy review. Pass.
